overview: Computing science graduate with 5+ years industry (software testing & development) and research experience
A fast learner who is experienced in working in team-centric and fast-paced environments
Proficient in C/C++, Python, TypeScript & Swift, and experience with cloud computing services like Amazon Web Service (AWS) & Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
Proficient in data analysis frameworks, such as NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib & SciPy

positions.title: Clinical Research Project Assistant
description: Examine the association between high-level moderation to physical activities \& social networking and less fatigue \& depression among youth with multiple sclerosis
Assess and implement consumer-grade wearables (e.g., Fitbit, Apple Watch) in pediatric clinical trials, focusing on technical considerations and practical applications

Develop and maintain a native iOS application, ATOMIC, designed to provide youth with multiple sclerosis access to information and support for a healthy, active lifestyle as part of an international research program
Develop and maintain a web-based data dashboard for clinical trials, enabling coaching staff and researchers to manipulate, visualize, and analyze real-time data and behaviours

positions.title: Research Software Developer
description: Work as part of an agile development team to create, maintain and optimize various software tools that support McMaster's researchers and research facilities
Contribute to open-source software projects that support research activities by triaging and patching issues

Derive principles and practices of research data management to develop institutional recommendations for research software management and planning, promoting FAIR principles, open science, and research reproducibility

Deliver expertise in software engineering within a research context by creating training materials and conducting workshop-style presentations on topics such as web accessibility, generative artificial intelligence and containerization for researchers

positions.title: Undergraduate Research Assistant
description: Investigated changes in central mass densities of galaxies during quenching and analyzed correlations with blackhole masses

Processed data from cosmological magnetohydrodynamical simulations (IllustrisTNG) to statistically visualize the compaction effect at the end of galaxy evolution using (SLURM-based) high-performance computing clusters
Hosted outreach activities to discuss research findings and engage with the regional astronomy community

positions.title: Software Developer
description: Diagnosed and resolved over 100 issues, assisting the customer support team with triaging customer and field issues
Developed and optimized front-end UI features based on engineering and UX requirements, primarily using Swift and Objective-C
Integrated business analytics tools and ensured user-data protection within various networking protocols (SOAP and REST APIs) in the application

positions.title: Certification Engineer (Co-op)
description: Managed configuration for multiple LTE and 5G mobile hotspot products, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements and creating customized packages for different carriers
Triaged and tracked customer issues, coordinating with firmware, software, and certification teams to troubleshoot problems in a fast-paced agile environment

Developed and maintained automated software tools and build scripts to enhance workflow processes, data analysis, and project testing in LTE Advanced Carrier Aggregation, using various languages and frameworks (e.g., Python, C\#, .NET framework, AT commands, Visual Basic)

experience.title: Resumpy
description: Developed Python scripts for automating clean document generation of resumes, CVs and cover letters in PDF format (this resume is generated using Resumpy)
Designed parsing mechanism of JSON data to generic document components and layout settings written in \LaTeX
Implemented the modules in the form of deployable containerized REST API service using Flask

experience.title: Stable Matching Quantum Algorithm
description: Utilized Grover’s algorithm and designed a quantum algorithm ($O(n\sqrt{n})$) which outperformed the classical Gale-Shapley stable matching algorithm ($O(n^2)$) in terms of time complexity
Researched and designed search black boxes for all entities in the proof of concept demo
Designed a method to compute the stability of all possible stable matches and return the most stable matching pair with an accuracy of 75\% on the Qiskit Aer quantum computing simulator

experience.title: Variable Star Photometry
description: Compiled a calibrated dataset from raw data with Python by reducing the effects of atmospheric extinction and cosmic rays with Python and several astrophysics packages (e.g. SEP)
Applied numerous operations on the dataset and derived meaningful information using domain knowledge in astrophysics (e.g. luminosity periodicity in variable stars)
Produced an observation proposal regarding the ideal target and telescope usage time calculation and a journal paper with methodology, calculation results and graphical results
